title: podman v3 container start fails when created via compat rest api
body:
### Issue Description

i have socat setting up tcp on a podman udp socket.

i am testing creating a container with the rest api:

```

curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{
"user": "root",
    "image": "listval",
    "name": "",
    "command": [
        "/bin/bash",
        "-c",
        "pipenv run python3 -m listval.main"
    ],
    "env": [
        "LIST_NAME=hello_world",
        "LIST_TAGS=[Tag1,Tag2]",
        "LIST_DESCRIPTION=bllahblah",
        "LIST_SQL_CODE=select * from bblahah"
    ],
    "volumes": {
        "/job/.env": {
            "type": "volume",
            "bind": "/home/admin/.ssh/.env",
            "mode": "0777"
        }
    },
    "portMappings": [
        {
            "containerPort": 5599,
            "hostPort": 5599
        }
    ],
    "remove": true
}' http://10.235.77.21:2930/containers/create


```
that creates a container for me with this kind of output: `{"Id":"19b6f416ca61c41cd125bc2e3cc115049791a95cc23ce77eba2fe47d7e71cab4","Warnings":[]}`

(incidentally, `name` does not give the container the name i want - it comes out random)

then i try to start the container and i get all sorts of `permission denied` in the log.

if i do the following based on the same image, it works:

```
podman run \
    --rm \
    --name list1 \
    -v /home/admin/.ssh/.env:/job/.env \
    -p 5599:5599 \
    --env 'LIST_NAME="blahblah"' \
    --env 'LIST_TAGS="[blah, blah2]"' \
    --env 'LIST_DESCRIPTION="testing podman envs"' \
    listval \
    /bin/bash -c "pipenv run python3 -m listval.main"

```



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. create a container with the rest api
2. start the container in command line
3. it won't keep running

I reproduced with the latest podman and found the issue.  Replace `command` with `cmd` in your JSON. Doing a `podman inspect` on the container, you should see that the specified command isn't used but the one from the container image.  That is because JSON unmarshaller ignored unknown keys, in this case `command`.

I am optimistically closing the issue, but please speak up if `s/command/cmd` does not solve the issue on your end.
You are correct; the structure we use (SpecGen) is documented, but not in a way such that our documentation generator can make REST API docs from it.

> You are correct; the structure we use (SpecGen) is documented

Is it available on docs.podman.io?

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/specgen/specgen.go#L561-L571

Individual structs in there are in the same file, and well-commented. Unfortunately, that's all the documentation available at the moment.

Unfortunately there's a lot of backend code behind this to generate the spec so bits like this can be hard to find. AFAIK we start with Entrypoint (if not provided, image Entrypoint is used instead) and append Command (again, if not provided, image CMD is used), which forms the actual command run in the container.
